examples.title: Return by Value with RVO
examples.description: Demonstrates how RVO eliminates copy operations when returning objects
examples.explanation: In C++17, RVO is mandatory. The Box object is constructed directly in the caller's stack frame, eliminating the copy/move constructor call. Even without RVO, copy elision may occur.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - single object in caller stack

examples.title: Return by Reference - Proper Usage
examples.description: Shows correct scenarios for returning by reference
examples.explanation: Returning references is efficient but must ensure the referenced object outlives the reference. Use const reference for read-only access and non-const for chaining. Static variables and member variables (via this) are safe to return by reference.

examples.title: Danger: Returning Reference to Local Variable
examples.explanation: Never return references or pointers to local automatic variables. The local variable is destroyed when the function returns, leaving a dangling reference. Use return by value for locals or heap-allocated memory with smart pointers for automatic management.

examples.title: Move Semantics and std::move
examples.description: Demonstrates efficient object return using move semantics
examples.explanation: Move semantics transfer ownership of resources (like heap-allocated memory) without copying. The move constructor transfers the pointer and sets the source to nullptr. std::move converts an lvalue to rvalue, forcing move construction. In modern C++, RVO often makes explicit std::move unnecessary.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - pointer transfer vs O(n) allocation

examples.title: Operator Chaining with References
examples.description: Shows how to implement chainable operators using references
examples.explanation: For operator chaining: compound operators (+=, -=) return reference to enable modification chaining. Binary operators (+, -) typically return new objects by value. Stream operators return stream references to allow (cout << a << b << c).
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for in-place, O(1) for new objects
